explanation: This 3-model ensemble achieves the highest holdout lower CI (0.022) — 54% improvement over the best single model (0.0143). The combination is optimal for several reasons: (1) It includes models with complementary approaches: t_learner_lgb (tree-based, non-linear), t_learner_ridge (linear, regularized), and hurdle (specialized for zero-inflated data). (2) Equal weighting provides robustness and avoids overfitting to CV performance. (3) None of these models were top CV performers (dr_learner had cv_lower_ci=0.0118), yet their ensemble excels on holdout, suggesting they capture different aspects of heterogeneous treatment effects. (4) The trio excludes dr_learner, r_learner, and x_learner which, despite strong CV metrics, don't improve holdout performance when combined.
alternatives_considered: t_learner_ridge+x_learner with gap_weights (0.022): Tied performance but only 2 models, less diversity
t_learner_ridge+x_learner with equal weights (0.0219): Nearly identical but slightly lower
t_learner_ridge+hurdle with rank_weights (0.0218): Lower CI, and rank weighting (67/33) seems arbitrary vs equal
Single hurdle model (0.0143): 35% worse than ensemble, lacks diversity protection
Single t_learner_ridge (0.0142): Similar issue to hurdle alone
All 6 models equal (0.0143): Adding dr_learner, r_learner, x_learner dilutes performance back to single-model levels
overfitting_assessment: LOW RISK. The selected ensemble shows strong generalization: (1) Gap between CV and holdout is reasonable — the component models had CV gaps of 0.0223-0.0242, suggesting stable estimation. (2) Equal weighting eliminates parameter tuning overfitting. (3) The 54% improvement over single models on holdout (not CV) indicates genuine complementarity, not memorization. (4) The fact that CV-best models (dr_learner) don't dominate holdout suggests this ensemble captured true signal diversity. (5) Consistency across strategies — the t_learner_ridge+x_learner pair also performed well with multiple weighting schemes, validating robustness.
